title: quercus sagraeana
intro: Quercus sagraeana, also known as Sagra's white oak or Sagra's oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to Cuba. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, attributed to the presence of various polyphenolic compounds. The health benefits of Quercus sagraeana are said to include the potential reduction of oxidative stress, improvement of cardiovascular health, and modulation of the immune system. Quercus sagraeana can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
